155→ Intended for use after PR submission or rewrite so that plans are correctly 156→ marked as being implemented even when the user bypasses the /erk:plan-implement 157→ pipeline. 158→ 159→ Silently returns on any failure — lifecycle updates must never block submission. 160→ """ 161→ plan_result = ctx.pr_backend.get_managed_pr(repo_root, pr_id) 162→ if isinstance(plan_result, PrNotFound): 163→ return 164→ 165→ current_stage = header_str(plan_result.header_fields, LIFECYCLE_STAGE) 166→ if current_stage not in _STAGES_BEFORE_IMPL: 167→ return 168→ 169→ try: 170→ ctx.pr_backend.update_metadata(repo_root, pr_id, {"lifecycle_stage": "impl"}) 171→ except RuntimeError as e: 172→ if not quiet: 173→ msg = f" Warning: failed to update lifecycle stage: {e}" 174→ click.echo(click.style(msg, fg="yellow")) 175→ return 176→ 177→ if not quiet: 178→ click.echo(click.style(" Plan lifecycle stage updated to impl", dim=True)) 179→

195→ this function reconstructs it from the plan's stored metadata so that 196→ ``assemble_pr_body`` can re-embed it in the PR body. 197→ 198→ Returns None if the plan cannot be found, allowing callers to proceed 199→ without a plan-header (current behavior). 200→ """ 201→ plan_result = ctx.pr_backend.get_managed_pr(repo_root, pr_id) 202→ if isinstance(plan_result, PrNotFound): 203→ return None 204→ 205→ # If the plan still has header_fields, use them directly 206→ if plan_result.header_fields: 207→ return MetadataBlock(key=BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER, data=dict(plan_result.header_fields)) 208→ 209→ # Otherwise, construct a minimal plan-header from PR metadata

## Summary
I've successfully renamed all occurrences in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py`:
**Changes made:** - **Line 161**: `ctx.plan_backend.get_managed_pr(...)` → `ctx.pr_backend.get_managed_pr(...)` - **Line 170**: `ctx.plan_backend.update_metadata(...)` → `ctx.pr_backend.update_metadata(...)` - **Line 201**: `ctx.plan_backend.get_managed_pr(...)` → `ctx.pr_backend.get_managed_pr(...)`
**Details:** - There were no explicit imports of `require_plan_backend` in this file, so no import statements needed updating - All 3 occurrences of `ctx.plan_backend` attribute access were updated to `ctx.pr_backend` - User-facing string messages and external API field names were preserved as instructed (e.g., "plan-header", "Plan lifecycle stage updated to impl", etc.) - Comments referencing the "plan backend" functionality were kept as-is since they describe behavior rather than being direct API references
